---
title: "JobAgeProject"
author: "Obydah Jazaeri, Sarah Rosen"
date: "2026-02-21"
output: html_document
runtime: shiny
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(shiny)
library(readxl)
library(tidyverse)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)

jobdata <- read_excel("cpsa2024.xlsx")
```



```{r eruptions, echo=FALSE}
inputPanel(
  selectInput("n_breaks", label = "Number of bins:",
              choices = c(10, 20, 35, 50), selected = 20),
  
  sliderInput("bw_adjust", label = "Bandwidth adjustment:",
              min = 0.2, max = 2, value = 1, step = 0.2)
)

renderPlot({
  hist(faithful$eruptions, probability = TRUE, breaks = as.numeric(input$n_breaks),
       xlab = "Duration (minutes)", main = "Geyser eruption duration")
  
  dens <- density(faithful$eruptions, adjust = input$bw_adjust)
  lines(dens, col = "blue")
})
```

```{r tabsets, echo=FALSE}
shinyAppDir(
  system.file("examples/06_tabsets", package = "shiny"),
  options = list(
    width = "100%", height = 550
  )
)
```

## 2019 11b Data

Represents employment by age group, given a drop-down menu for individual age groups.
```{r import, echo=FALSE}
df_2019 <- read_excel("cpsaat11b-2019.xlsx", skip = 4)

head(df_2019)

colnames(df_2019)

colnames(df_2019)[1] <- "Occupation"

colnames(df_2019) <- gsub("\\r\\n", " ", colnames(df_2019))

colnames(df_2019)

df_2019 <- df_2019[, -c(2, ncol(df_2019))]

df_2019_tidy <- df_2019 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Occupation, 
    names_to = "age_group",
    values_to = "employed"
  ) %>%
  mutate (
    year = 2019,
    employed = as.numeric(employed)
  )
glimpse(df_2019_tidy)
```

```{r, echo=FALSE}
inputPanel(
  selectInput("age", "Select Age Group:",
              choices = unique(df_2019_tidy$age_group))
)

renderPlot({
  df_2019_tidy %>%
    filter(age_group == input$age) %>%
    slice_max(employed, n = 10) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = reorder(Occupation, employed),
               y = employed)) +
    geom_col() +
    coord_flip()
})
```
